questions.question: What is a community?
questions.correct_answer: A group of people with common interests or region
questions.explanation: Community can be defined in several ways and has many kinds

questions.question: Why do families support each other?
questions.explanation: Families are connected not only within themselves but also with others

questions.question: What is the role of individuals in a community?
questions.correct_answer: Specific duties to perform for the community's smooth functioning
questions.explanation: Individuals have responsibilities to maintain harmony in the community

questions.question: What type of family is limited to a couple and their children?
questions.explanation: A nuclear family consists of parents and their dependent children

questions.question: Who are often called a community in some contexts?
questions.explanation: Community can be referred to as jāti or its subdivision in certain situations

questions.question: What is the halma tradition about?
questions.correct_answer: Serving Mother Earth and community's needs
questions.explanation: The Bhil community's halma tradition focuses on serving their environment and community

questions.question: How do communities come together in times of crisis?
questions.correct_answer: By supporting each other through shared efforts
questions.explanation: Communities work together to address challenges and provide aid when needed

questions.question: What is an example of a new type of community that has emerged?
questions.correct_answer: Residents' Welfare Associations in urban areas
questions.explanation: These associations create rules and regulations for the benefit of their members

questions.question: Why are communities considered interdependent?
questions.correct_answer: Everyone depends on a number of other people and communities
questions.explanation: Communities rely on each other's contributions to function smoothly

questions.question: What is the result of not following duties within a community?
questions.correct_answer: The community will not function smoothly
questions.explanation: Each member has specific responsibilities to maintain harmony in the community

important_notes.title: Definition of Community
important_notes.content: A community is a group of people with common interests or region, defined in several ways and having many kinds.

important_notes.title: Family Support
important_notes.content: Families support each other due to love, care, cooperation, and interdependence, highlighting their connections beyond themselves.

important_notes.title: Role of Individuals in a Community
important_notes.content: Individuals have specific duties to perform for the community's smooth functioning, showcasing their responsibilities towards maintaining harmony.

important_notes.title: Nuclear Family
important_notes.content: A nuclear family consists of parents and their dependent children, being limited to this structure.

important_notes.title: Jāti or Subdivision of it as a Community
important_notes.content: In some contexts, jāti or its subdivision is referred to as a community, indicating the flexibility in defining community.

important_notes.title: Halma Tradition
important_notes.content: The halma tradition focuses on serving Mother Earth and addressing community needs, showcasing communal efforts for mutual benefit.

important_notes.title: Community Support during Crises
important_notes.content: Communities come together in times of crisis by supporting each other through shared efforts, demonstrating their unity in the face of challenges.

important_notes.title: Residents' Welfare Associations
important_notes.content: These associations create rules and regulations for the benefit of their members, illustrating new types of communities that have emerged.

important_notes.title: Interdependence within Communities
important_notes.content: Communities are considered interdependent as everyone relies on other people and communities to function smoothly.

important_notes.title: Consequences of Non-Performance of Community Duties
important_notes.content: If duties within a community are not followed, the community will not function smoothly, highlighting the importance of fulfilling responsibilities towards maintaining harmony.

facts.fact: The Bhil community planted thousands of trees in hundreds of villages to address an acute water crisis, following their halma tradition.
During the Chennai floods of 2015, private groups cooked large quantities of food and distributed it to people who needed it, showcasing community efforts during crises.
Shri Mahesh Sharma was honoured with the Padma Shri award for his transformational work with the Bhil communities in 2019.
Residents' Welfare Associations are examples of new types of communities that have emerged, making their own rules and regulations for urban areas.
The halma tradition aims to serve Mother Earth and address community needs, demonstrating the importance of communal efforts towards mutual benefit.
Communities are interdependent as everyone relies on a number of other people and communities to function smoothly, highlighting their interconnectedness.
If duties within a community are not followed, the community will not function smoothly, emphasizing the importance of fulfilling responsibilities towards maintaining harmony.
Indian languages have many terms for family relationships, but often lack specific words for 'cousin', highlighting the close bonds among all children in the family.
In some contexts, jāti or its subdivision is referred to as a community, illustrating the flexibility in defining community.
The Bhil community's halma tradition focuses on serving Mother Earth and addressing community needs, showcasing communal efforts for mutual benefit.
